name: Third-Person Camera
description: A camera viewpoint that follows behind, above, or beside the player avatar while keeping the character visible.
design_notes: Third-person cameras are a negotiation between avatar readability, environment visibility, aiming, and collision. Good tuning defines distance, shoulder side, recentering, occlusion handling, and how much control the player retains during combat or traversal.
design_purpose: Show the avatar and nearby threats while supporting movement and spatial awareness.
Frame melee combat, traversal, or exploration without requiring constant manual camera correction.
Give players a readable sense of character posture, animation, and surroundings.
player_fantasy: The player feels connected to an on-screen character while still seeing enough of the world to plan movement.

parameters.name: follow_distance
parameters.description: Preferred camera distance from the avatar pivot.

parameters.name: shoulder_offset
parameters.description: Horizontal and vertical offset used for over-the-shoulder framing.

parameters.name: occlusion_probe_radius
parameters.description: Radius used to keep the camera out of geometry.

parameters.name: recenter_delay_ms
parameters.description: Delay after player camera input before automatic recentering resumes.

parameters.name: orbit_degrees_per_second
parameters.description: Maximum camera orbit rate from player input or auto-follow.

edge_cases: Tight corridors should shorten camera distance without hiding the avatar or rapidly popping.
Aiming, lock-on, climbing, swimming, and vehicles can all request different third-person profiles.
Camera collision should ignore small foliage but respect walls, ceilings, and large moving objects.
Switching shoulder side must account for cover, walls, reticles, and input expectations.
common_bugs: Camera snaps when occlusion clears because the desired distance is restored instantly.
Avatar blocks the reticle or interact prompt at common shoulder offsets.
Recenter fights player input because manual-control cooldown is too short.
Collision probes use a ray instead of a volume and let the near plane clip through corners.
balancing_notes: Closer cameras increase tension and character presence; farther cameras improve awareness and traversal planning.
Combat cameras should preserve threat visibility without automatically solving positioning for the player.
accessibility_notes: Expose orbit sensitivity, inversion, recenter strength, shoulder side, and camera distance where possible.
Offer reduced camera acceleration and motion options for players sensitive to rapid orbiting.

implementation_notes.unity: Use a camera rig or Cinemachine virtual camera with separate aim, follow, and collision stages; apply occlusion before final damping to avoid pops.
implementation_notes.godot: Use a SpringArm3D or custom boom with collision checks, then rotate an orbit pivot from input while blending profile offsets for combat and traversal.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use SpringArmComponent camera collision and PlayerCameraManager profiles; separate control rotation from character facing when strafing or lock-on is active.
implementation_notes.web: Store orbit yaw, pitch, desired distance, and actual collision-limited distance in camera state, then interpolate the actual camera position each frame.

example_games.title: God of War (2018)
example_games.note: Uses a close third-person camera for combat and character presence.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto IV
example_games.note: Uses a third-person camera for on-foot movement, driving, and combat framing.

example_games.title: Grand Theft Auto V
example_games.note: Uses a third-person camera for on-foot movement, driving, and combat framing.

example_games.title: Red Dead Redemption 2
example_games.note: Uses third-person camera framing for on-foot exploration, riding, and combat.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses a third-person camera for exploration, horseback travel, and combat framing.

example_games.title: Gran Turismo 7
example_games.note: Uses chase-camera views for vehicle framing during races.

example_games.title: Need for Speed: Hot Pursuit
example_games.note: Uses chase-camera framing for high-speed pursuit driving.

example_games.title: Gears of War
example_games.note: Uses an over-the-shoulder third-person camera for cover-based firefights.

example_games.title: Fortnite
example_games.note: Uses third-person camera framing for movement, aiming, and building awareness.
